hook: You wouldn't leave a fire unattended for twenty years and be surprised it went out.
caption:
You wouldn't leave a fire unattended for twenty years and be surprised it went out.
But that's what we do with our relationships.
We throw everything on the pile, the kids, the careers, the mortgage, the mothers-in-law, the school runs, the deadlines that don't stop coming, and we expect love to keep burning on its own.
Then one morning we look across the kitchen at your partner and wonder where the spark went.
You just stopped feeding.
Maybe it's time to put some firewood on.

How can you fan the fire of your relationship?
question: How can you fan the fire of your relationship?

hook: No partner can fill a woman who won't fill herself.
caption:
If you are running on empty and wondering why your partner's not filling you up, read this twice.
No partner can fill a woman who won't fill herself.
Your love tank is yours to fill.
The dance on the kitchen floor at 6am. The walk by the water with your phone on silent. The long bath. The uninterrupted breakfast. The hour of reading in the sun. The girlfriend who makes you laugh until you cry.
These aren't luxuries, beautiful. They're oil for your lamp.
And when you show up to your partner full, they feel it.

What was the last thing you did that filled your own tank?
question: What was the last thing you did that filled your own tank?

hook: Your yearning is not your weakness. It's your superpower.
caption:
You've been told to dim it your whole life.
Don't want too much. Don't ask for too much. Don't be too emotional. Don't be too sexual. Don't be too much.
And so you shrank yourself into a manageable version. Polite. Capable. Reliable.
And then you wondered why you felt invisible.
Beautiful, listen.
Your yearning is not your weakness. It's your superpower.
The depth you've been hiding is the exact thing that makes you magnetic.
Show it. Let it be seen. Not everyone can handle it, and that's the point.

Drop a fire emoji if you've felt this one.
question: Drop a fire emoji if you've felt this one.

hook: You can only change your relationship if you decide so.
caption:
You can only change your relationship if you decide so.
Not when your partner changes. Not when the kids grow up. Not when work calms down.
The moment you decide.
This week's practice from the book. Take five minutes a day and answer these out loud.
1. What has it cost me to not take my share of responsibility for the state of my relationship?
2. Who has it hurt, and how badly?
3. How has it hurt me?
4. What do I lose when I feel helpless and blame others?
Then claim your power: I'm powerful. I can create life. I can create the relationship I desire. The power is in my hands.

Save this post. Try the practice.
question: Save this post. Try the practice.

theme: Hurt Little Girl vs Your Truth
hook: Your partner didn't text back. And within three minutes you had written a whole novel in your head.
caption:
Your partner didn't text back.
And within three minutes you had written a whole novel in your head.
They don't care. They're pulling away. They're probably with someone else. They've never loved you properly. You should leave first.
That's not your adult talking. That's your hurt little girl. The one who decided around age six that she wasn't enough.
Every time you let her drive, she finds the evidence. Because you make it happen.
Your adult secure self doesn't need a text back to know she's loved.

Who's driving your next move, her or the little one?
question: Who's driving your next move?

hook: The fastest way to watch your partner go quiet is to make them your next improvement project.
caption:
The fastest way to watch your partner go quiet is to make them your next improvement project.
They can feel it before you even open your mouth.
The tight little smile. The when you have a minute, can we talk. The list in your head of all the things they could be doing better. The article you sent about emotional availability.
They're not a project, beautiful. They're your partner.
They want to make you happy. They've just stopped believing they can.
Appreciate the effort, not the outcome. Catch them getting it right, not getting it wrong.

Appreciation or criticism, which one have you been feeding them this week?
question: Appreciation or criticism this week?

hook: Somebody somewhere decided that sex without orgasm is a failure.
caption:
Somebody somewhere decided that sex without orgasm is a failure. And then they sold us that lie for fifty years.
Beautiful, nobody is keeping score.
Your yoni doesn't hand out trophies at the end. Your body doesn't care about the finish line. Your nervous system cares about one thing, do you feel met?
Do you feel present? Do you feel held? Do you feel like a woman instead of an object?
Orgasm is lovely when it happens. But it is not the point.
Stop chasing. Start feeling.

Tag the girlfriend who needs to hear this today.
question: Tag the girlfriend who needs to hear this today.

hook: When your partner disconnects, where does your mind go?
caption:
When your partner disconnects, where does your mind go?
Mine used to go straight back to age six. Not wanted. Not enough.
Until I discovered my truth.
This week's practice. Find quiet time.
1. Remember a recent moment of tension. See it in detail.
2. Feel it in your body. Name the feeling without judging it.
3. Ask: Where do I feel this? How old is this feeling?
4. Remember a moment you were head over heels in love. From that place, ask: who am I really?
5. Write three statements that contradict the old beliefs. I'm loved. I'm more than enough. I'm a blessing to life.

Which one of yours is the strongest?
question: Which one of yours is the strongest?

hook: When was the last time you said out loud what you wanted?
caption:
When was the last time you said out loud what you wanted?
Not hinted. Not suggested. Said it.
Slower. Here. Look at me. I want you to.
Most of us were raised to not take up space.
And then we turn 45 and wonder why your partner still doesn't know what we like, after twenty years of sharing a bed.
Your partner's not a mind reader, beautiful. They love you. They would absolutely love it if you told them what you wanted.
Your voice is your power. In the kitchen. In the boardroom. Especially in the bedroom.

What do you wish you could say out loud this week?
question: What do you wish you could say out loud this week?

hook: If you can't say out loud what you want from love, the universe doesn't know what to bring you.
caption:
If you can't say out loud what you want from love, the universe doesn't know what to bring you.
Most women over 40 know exactly what they don't want.
But when I ask what they actually want, they go quiet.
Get specific, beautiful.
Not I want my partner to be more loving. That's a fog.
I want my partner to walk in the door and find me first. I want long Sundays in bed talking about nothing. I want to be kissed at the kitchen sink. I want sex that takes its time. I want to be cherished out loud.
That's an intention.
Write it down. Read it daily. Draw a symbol for it.

Drop one word from your intention in the comments.
question: Drop one word from your intention.

hook: You cannot be the manager of your partner's life and their lover at the same time.
caption:
You cannot be the manager of your partner's life and their lover at the same time.
You booked the holiday. You read the parenting book. You scheduled the in-laws. You remember which kid has the dentist Thursday.
And then on Saturday night, you wonder why they're not reaching for you.
Beautiful, they don't know how to switch the magnet back on either.
The energy that runs the household is not the energy that creates desire.
Drop the clipboard for one evening. Let them drive, plan dinner, be the partner.

Manager or lover this week, which one have you been?
question: Manager or lover this week?

caption:
What makes your eyes shine?
Not what you should be doing. Not what's on the to-do list.
This week's practice from the book. Design your Extreme Self-Care programme.
1. Movement that brings you joy
2. Healthy, slow, sensual eating
3. Sleep, in bed by ten
4. Time with girlfriends
5. Time alone and spiritual practice
6. Time with nature
7. Creative expression
Pick one from each. Put it in the diary. Not aspirational, in the diary.
Fill your love tanks first. Then share from the overflow.

Which of the seven are you saying yes to this week?
question: Which of the seven this week?

hook: When was the last time you looked at her?
caption:
When was the last time you looked at her?
Not in the shower. Not in passing. Properly. With a hand-held mirror, the door locked, no agenda.
Most of us never have.
We were taught to be repulsed by the most sacred part of us. Taught she was dirty. Taught not to touch.
And then we wonder why sex feels disconnected.
You cannot have a great sex life with a part of your body you've never met.
This week, beautiful, meet her.
Lock the door. Get the mirror. Take a breath. Look. Say hi.

Have you ever done this practice?
question: Have you ever done this practice?

hook: If you're waiting to feel like having sex, you're going to keep waiting.
caption:
If you're waiting to feel like having sex, you're going to keep waiting.
For years, beautiful. Possibly forever.
Somebody sold us the idea that real desire is spontaneous. That if you have to plan it, something is broken.
This is the lie that quietly ended a generation of marriages.
Women over 40, dual careers, kids, perimenopause. The spontaneous desire window is small and getting smaller.
Put it in the diary.
Friday night. Sunday morning. Pick the window, defend it like a board meeting, dress up.
Arousal follows the action, not the other way round.

What's stopping you from booking it this week?
question: What's stopping you from booking it?

hook: Before you touch each other tonight, beautiful, breathe together.
caption:
Before you touch each other tonight, beautiful, breathe together.
It sounds too simple to matter. It changes everything.
We rush into sex the same way we rush into the rest of life. Five minutes from arguing about the dishwasher to trying to make love.
Sit facing your partner. Knees touching. Hands resting. Look into their left eye. Breathe in for four. Out for four. Sync your breath.
Three minutes. That's all.
The armour comes off. The to-do list dissolves. Your heart rates synchronise.
Then the touch lands somewhere completely different.

Tag the girlfriend who needs this reminder.
question: Tag the girlfriend who needs this.

theme: Practice: Tune In to Your Partner's Inner World
hook: Deep inside every partner there is a hero who desperately wants to succeed with you.
caption:
Deep inside every partner there is a hero who desperately wants to succeed with you.
Your partner needs to feel significant. If they don't feel significant in their own home, they retreat into the phone, the screen, the garage, the silence.
This week's practice from the book. Tune in.
Find quiet time. Close your eyes. Imagine stepping into your partner's body. Answer as if you are them.
1. What do you feel about her?
2. Do you feel loved by her?
3. What do you want from her?
4. Do you have any fears about her?
Let the answers come. They will surprise you.
Then every evening this week, write three things you're grateful for about them. Say thank you, out loud, to their face.

What's one thing you appreciate about them today?
question: What's one thing you appreciate today?
